disease_association.detail: Strong genetic and literature evidence linking TGFBR1 to IPF pathogenesis via TGF-beta-driven fibroblast activation and extracellular matrix deposition.

druggability.detail: Serine/threonine kinase receptor. Well-established kinase target class with extensive medicinal chemistry precedent.

chemical_matter.detail: Substantial chemical matter exists. Multiple chemotypes with sub-100nM potency. Hinge binder motifs well characterized.

clinical_precedent.detail: Multiple ALK5 inhibitors have reached Phase II-III. Galunisertib tested in IPF (Phase II). Vactosertib in clinical trials for fibrotic diseases.

structural_data.detail: Multiple co-crystal structures available (e.g. PDB 1VJY). Hinge region well resolved. Suitable for structure-based drug design.

safety_signals.evidence: TGF-beta signaling regulates immune homeostasis, wound healing, and tumor suppression. Complete pathway inhibition in preclinical models caused cardiac valve lesions and immune dysregulation.
safety_signals.mitigation: Selective ALK5 inhibition over ALK4/ALK7. Controlled exposure via intermittent dosing. Safety-first compound design prioritizing selectivity.

safety_signals.evidence: TGF-beta has dual role: tumor suppressor in early stages, tumor promoter in late stages. Long-term ALK5 inhibition has theoretical cancer risk, but no confirmed clinical signal to date.
safety_signals.mitigation: Monitor for neoplasia in long-term studies. Consider indication-specific benefit-risk.
